title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 24

thm:1.1.summary: The Leech lattice achieves the optimal sphere packing density in $\mathbb{R}^{24}$, and it is the only periodic packing in $\mathbb{R}^{24}$ with that density, up to scaling and isometries — the dimension-24 analogue of Viazovska's $E_8$ theorem.

thm:1.2.summary: Restatement of the Cohn–Elkies LP bound: for Schwartz $f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}$ with $f(0)=\widehat{f}(0)=1$, $f(x)\le 0$ for $|x|\ge r$, and $\widehat{f}(y)\ge 0$ for all $y$, the sphere packing density in $\mathbb{R}^n$ is at most $(r/2)^n$ times the volume of the unit ball. Imported from Cohn–Elkies (math/0110009).

lem:2.1.summary: The dim-24 $+1$-eigenfunction $a$ is a radial Schwartz function on $\mathbb{R}^{24}$ with $\widehat{a}=a$, analytically continuing from a contour-integral definition involving quasi-modular forms — the dim-24 analogue of the $+1$-eigenfunction in Viazovska's $E_8$ construction.

lem:3.1.summary: The dim-24 $-1$-eigenfunction $b$ is a radial Schwartz function on $\mathbb{R}^{24}$ with $\widehat{b}=-b$, defined as a quasi-modular contour integral — the dim-24 analogue of Viazovska's $-1$-eigenfunction.

lem:\\upshapeA.1.summary: Quasi-modular-form inequality used in the sign check: for $t>0$, $\varphi(it) < 0$ — one of the explicit estimates establishing the magic function's sign conditions.

lem:\\upshapeA.2.summary: Quasi-modular-form inequality: for $t>0$, $\varphi(it) - \frac{432}{\pi^2}\psi_S(it) > 0$ — a second sign estimate used in verifying the magic-function conditions.

lem:\\upshapeA.3.summary: Growth inequality: for $t\ge 1$, $B(t) > \frac{1}{39}t e^{2\pi t} - \frac{10}{117\pi}e^{2\pi t}$ — the asymptotic estimate completing the magic-function sign argument.

edges.note: The proof applies the Cohn–Elkies LP bound (Theorem 1.2) at radius $r=2$ (the minimum vector length of the Leech lattice).
The Leech magic function is a linear combination of the $+1$-eigenfunction $a$ (Lemma 2.1) and the $-1$-eigenfunction $b$ (Lemma 3.1).
The Leech magic function uses the $-1$-eigenfunction $b$ (Lemma 3.1).
The sign condition $f\le 0$ for $|x|\ge 2$ is verified using the inequality $\varphi(it)<0$ (Lemma A.1).
The sign condition uses the inequality $\varphi(it) - \frac{432}{\pi^2}\psi_S(it) > 0$ (Lemma A.2).
The asymptotic sign argument uses the lower bound on $B(t)$ for large $t$ (Lemma A.3).

main_result.description: The headline: the Leech lattice is the densest packing in $\mathbb{R}^{24}$.

eigenfunctions.description: The dim-24 $+1$ and $-1$ Fourier eigenfunctions $a$ and $b$ from quasi-modular contour integrals — the Leech analogue of Viazovska's $E_8$ eigenfunctions.

quasimodular_inequalities.description: Explicit estimates on quasi-modular forms verifying the magic function's sign conditions.

CE.title: New upper bounds on sphere packings I (Cohn–Elkies)

Viazovska-E8.title: The sphere packing problem in dimension 8 (Viazovska)
